task.title: Dispatch registered tools durably for active tasks
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/554 by composing stable-ID registry lookup with the existing task associated durable invocation protocol.
acceptance_criteria: Unknown IDs fail before durable intent, task validation, authorization, or adapter execution.
Known IDs delegate exactly once to the existing task associated durable path.
Task preconditions, duplicate protection, permission decisions, metadata-only retention, outcomes, exact successful output, and source chains remain unchanged.
No provider parsing, runtime orchestration, concrete adapter, retry, or second persistence protocol is added.

attempts.summary: Chose composition before provider tool-call orchestration.
attempts.diagnostic: Provider parsing would have joined two caller-side mechanisms before registry dispatch and durable task evidence shared a single entry point.
attempts.patch: Decision issue 553 selected registry lookup followed by the existing durable task invocation protocol; execution issue 554 fixed unknown-ID ordering, error preservation, and non-goals.

attempts.summary: Established RED with durable registry acceptance tests.
attempts.diagnostic: The registry integration target failed because the durable dispatch method and its typed wrapper error did not exist.
attempts.patch: Kept cases for known allow and deny, adapter failure, missing and inactive tasks, duplicate invocation IDs, exact output, source chains, and unknown IDs with no durable or authorization side effects.

attempts.diagnostic: ToolRegistry resolves a mutable adapter first and returns typed NotFound when absent. Known adapters are passed as trait objects to invoke_tool_for_task_durable, preserving one implementation of validation, persistence, authorization, execution, and terminal handling.
attempts.patch: Added DurableToolRegistryInvocationError and ToolRegistry::invoke_for_task_durable, relaxed the existing wrapper to accept unsized Tool trait objects, expanded registry tests, and documented the composed ordering.

attempts.summary: Verified, reviewed independently, and merged the composition slice.
attempts.diagnostic: Pinned just verify, focused registry and task association tests, and rustdoc with warnings denied passed. Independent pre-commit review found no actionable issue. Rust quality gate run 30058584544 succeeded; CodeRabbit reported adaptive review limiting instead of a new line review.
attempts.patch: Pull request 555 squash-merged as 9f69fabaa27886973766d9306ee497b380532a43 and closed issue 554.

outcome.summary: Registered adapters can now be resolved by stable ID and executed through the established durable active-task permission protocol without duplicate orchestration logic.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test tool_registry
nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test tool_task_association
Independent pre-commit review # no actionable findings

lessons: Resolve unknown adapter IDs before creating durable intent so nonexistent tools leave no audit stream that resembles attempted execution.
Composition should delegate to the established durable path rather than duplicate task, permission, retention, or crash semantics.
Trait-object support can be the only generic change needed to connect registry ownership with an existing adapter protocol.
Provider parsing and runtime loops should follow only after registry dispatch and durable task execution form one stable callable boundary.
